heroSubtitle: Experience the soul of Trieste. High-quality coffee, exclusively roasted with beechwood according to an ancient 18th-century tradition.
seasonalDescription: A curated selection of blends—each designed as a sensory masterpiece, shaped by flame, time, and the Triestine palate.
tickerText: TRADITIONAL BEECH WOOD ROASTING — CAFFÈ ATT — TRIESTE — TRADITION SINCE 1892
editorialBody: Caffè ATT’s process is a deliberate rejection of industrial haste. Each batch is guided by master roasters who listen for the distinct "first crack" and watch for the subtle shift in hue that signals perfection.
editorialPoint1Body: Born in the City of Coffee—where the port meets the palate—this brand carries a culture that refined espresso into an art form.
editorialPoint2Body: Beyond sustainability, the commitment extends into regenerative practices so the earth continues to yield exceptional coffee cherries for generations.
woodWhyBody: Unlike contemporary gas roasters, a time-honored beechwood fire imparts a gentle, humid heat that penetrates the core of each bean. This ancient method preserves delicate essential oils and cultivates an aromatic complexity unattainable through industrial processes. It is a harmonious dance between fire and bean—culminating in a cup that is intensely flavorful, exquisitely velvety, and profoundly balanced.
woodCard1Body: Beechwood embers create a softer thermal curve, helping preserve the delicate oils and aromas inside the bean.
woodCard2Body: After roasting, the coffee rests and matures before blending—allowing structure, sweetness, and crema to harmonize.
sommelierSubtitle: Let our digital connoisseur curate a coffee ritual based on your mood and time of day.
sommelierFallbackDescription: A dark, velvety cup with a persistent cream—excellent for espresso and milk drinks alike.
footerTagline: Caffè ATT — authentic Trieste coffee, roasted over beechwood since 1892. Represented in Lithuania by Koliziejus.
koliziejusNote: Official Caffè ATT representative in Lithuania. Original Italian products.
b2bDescription: We are eager to collaborate with cafes, restaurants, and companies. We offer favorable terms and individual solutions.
newsletterDescription: Get updates on seasonal blends, limited editions, and coffee rituals from Trieste.
newsletterNote: Newsletter sent once a month. Unsubscribe anytime.
companyDescription: Caffè ATT — authentic Trieste coffee roasted over beechwood. Represented in Lithuania by Koliziejus.
aboutIntro: Caffè ATT (Antica Tostatura Triestina) transcends the mere act of roasting coffee; it creates coffee-flavored art. The philosophy is rooted in the conviction that an exceptional cup of espresso is a multisensory masterpiece—crafted to bridge nature’s raw elegance and the pinnacle of artisanal skill.
philosophyBody: Chosen and presented in Lithuania by Koliziejus, Caffè ATT stands for heritage, emotion, and responsibility—crafted in Trieste for those who seek depth in every cup.
philosophyPillar1Body: A devoted custodian of an 18th-century Triestine tradition—the ancient art of wood-fired roasting. In an era of automation, the craft remains guided by human expertise and the natural warmth of the flame.
philosophyPillar2Body: Each blend is conceived as a work of art—designed to evoke profound emotion through intense aromatic nuance, stirring memories and creating new experiences.
philosophyPillar3Body: A commitment that extends beyond sustainability—embracing regenerative practices so the earth continues to yield extraordinary coffee cherries for generations.
philosophyPillar4Body: Born in Trieste—the globally recognized City of Coffee—embodying the sophisticated culture of a historic port where centuries of trade shaped the world’s finest coffee traditions.
storyTitle: From the Historic Port of Trieste to Your Cup
storySection1Body: The story begins in the 1700s in Trieste, Italy—a pivotal Mediterranean gateway for the global coffee trade. Through generations of experimentation, artisans perfected roasting over slow-burning beechwood fires, defining the Triestine method as a gold standard for depth and character.
storySection2Body: In 1995, Alessandro Hausbrandt—descendant of one of Italy’s legendary coffee families—founded Antica Tostatura Triestina to resurrect the authentic taste of Italian espresso by returning to artisanal roots and rekindling the Eternal Flame of wood-roasting.
storySection3Body: For over two decades, the roastery has served as a laboratory of sensory exploration. Only the finest green coffees are selected from Africa, the Americas, and India, then brought to Trieste to meet the beechwood fire. Master roasters listen for the precise crack and watch for the perfect shade of mahogany.
storySection4Body: This dedication to slow coffee has not gone unnoticed. The New York Times famously hailed the espresso as the "Best in New York"—proof that the soul of Trieste can travel across oceans.
storySection5Body: Koliziejus brings this legacy to Lithuania by representing Caffè ATT and making its blends available in Vilnius. It is an invitation to step beyond the ordinary and experience a moment of coffee-flavored art—each sip a tribute to centuries of tradition and passion.
